authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-11, Section 280: Managing Customer Experience and Improving Service Delivery
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB sets executive-branch management policy; Section 280 is an authoritative federal baseline for public-service journey, service-standard, feedback, and customer-facing program workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program intake, service-state tracking, participant communications, and service-standard administration

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 551-559: Administrative Procedure Act provisions
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. Code text for federal administrative procedure, including definitions and process guardrails that distinguish administrative support from official adjudication or agency action.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authority classification, notice-sensitive handling, written-denial routing, and adjudication boundary control
used_for: adjudication and official agency-action refusal scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Government-wide federal rule for award-funded public programs, including internal controls, procurement standards, reporting, record retention, record access, remedies, hearings, appeals, closeout, and audit requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program record validation, reporting, procurement-adjacent file checks, retention, access, remedies, and audit-ready evidence handling
used_for: due-process escalation boundaries for remedies, hearings, and appeals

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority and publishes official records-management, scheduling, disposition, transfer, and electronic-record guidance for agency records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official-record identification, retention, disposition, transfer, and records governance escalation
used_for: recordkeeping expectations for program decisions and correspondence

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552: Public information; agency rules, opinions, orders, records, and proceedings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. Code text for FOIA that governs federal agency record availability and constrains program administrator handling of transparency or disclosure requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-records, transparency-log, disclosure, and access-boundary handling
used_for: refusal to make disclosure or withholding decisions

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552a: Records maintained on individuals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Privacy Act text that governs federal records about individuals and constrains participant, applicant, beneficiary, and member record access, amendment, and disclosure handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Participant-data privacy, consent, access, amendment, and disclosure-boundary handling
used_for: refusal to disclose protected records without authority

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 4.801 - Government Contract Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Acquisition.GOV publishes the current FAR; FAR 4.801 defines contract-file documentation as the complete transaction history supporting acquisition decisions, reviews, investigations, litigation, and congressional inquiries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement-document readiness, contract-file evidence, and procurement authority routing
used_for: refusal to certify or approve procurement decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for documentation, control activities, segregation of duties, monitoring, risk, and auditability expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control documentation, segregation of duties, owner-state tracking, audit trail, monitoring, and exception escalation
used_for: segregation of authority and owner-state expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 205.10 - Hearings
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal public-assistance hearing rule that anchors notice, opportunity to be heard, and rights-sensitive escalation behavior when program administration touches benefits or services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Notice, hearing, appeal-window, and rights-sensitive public-service escalation
used_for: refusal to interpret or decide rights

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request type, program or membership lifecycle stage, requested decision authority, tenant prerequisites, owner map, and adjacent-specialist needs before taking record action.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Program context and tenant fact collection
workflow_stages.description: Collect jurisdiction, agency, business unit, program statute, public guidance, service population, due-process constraints, source hierarchy, authority map, and systems-of-record references.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Record and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Gather applications, roster records, eligibility files, permit records, notices, correspondence, procurement documents, public-service decisions, transparency logs, reporting schedules, and audit logs.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Validation and maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Check administrative completeness, currency, classification, source-of-truth alignment, owner state, due dates, required attachments, and system links without deciding the underlying public outcome.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Status, reporting, and evidence packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare program status summaries, document completeness summaries, administrative status notes, evidence packets, reporting trackers, record update notes, and audit-trail summaries tied to named records.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, handoff, publication, and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate retention, privacy, access, public-records, procurement, due-process, eligibility, adjudication, enforcement, legal, audit, financial-approval, and source-conflict issues to the correct owner.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

artifacts: Program charter, statute, public guidance, or agency manual reference
Tenant source hierarchy, authority map, and owner map
Participant, member, applicant, permittee, vendor, or recipient roster
Applications, enrollment records, membership records, eligibility files, and case files
Permit records, benefit records, service-request records, notices, hearing or appeal records, and public-service decisions
Submission receipts, system timestamps, correspondence logs, and status registers
Procurement documents, solicitation references, contract-file indexes, vendor communications, and procurement owner notes
Reporting calendars, performance or service reports, due-date trackers, and blocked approval logs
Transparency logs, public-records references, privacy or consent records, access logs, and retention schedules
Record update notes, audit logs, exception logs, escalation notes, and handoff packages
systems_of_record: Permits, licensing, or benefits administration systems
Identity, consent, privacy, and access-control systems

allowed: Classify public-sector program administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether required tenant facts, official records, attachments, owner facts, source context, retention inputs, and system links are present for administrative use.
Prepare record update notes, completeness summaries, administrative status notes, program or membership status summaries, evidence packets, audit-trail summaries, and escalation packages.
Recommend next administrative steps and owner routing without claiming official eligibility, benefit, permit, procurement, enforcement, legal, public-records, audit, or financial approval authority.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, program statute, agency policy, service population, authority map, records schedule, system-of-record path, or owner facts are missing.
Statutes, agency manuals, tenant policies, public guidance, records schedules, procurement rules, privacy rules, or system-of-record data materially conflict.

Eligibility, rights, benefits, permits, services, obligations, notices, hearings, appeals, enforcement, public-records disclosure, privacy, audit opinion, procurement authority, or financial approval affects the requested outcome.
Record retention, access, publication, deletion, transfer, amendment, or disposition cannot be resolved from documented authority.
refuse: Request asks the lane to approve, deny, revoke, suspend, sanction, enforce, award, waive, terminate, release, withhold, delete, or dispose of an official public-sector decision or record.

Request asks for legal interpretation, eligibility decision, hearing or appeal outcome, enforcement posture, procurement approval, audit opinion, financial approval, or public-records disclosure decision.
Required records, tenant facts, or authoritative context are missing and would change the outcome.
Stakeholder asks to alter official program records without documented delegated authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and auditable change trail.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative completeness as eligibility, benefit, permit, program participation, procurement, enforcement, legal, audit, or public-records approval.
Changing official program, roster, permit, notice, or eligibility records without delegated authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and audit trail.
Proceeding when jurisdiction, program statute, agency policy, service population, due-process rules, or tenant source hierarchy is missing or conflicting.
Publishing, deleting, withholding, or disclosing program records without resolving retention, privacy, public-records, access, or protected-information constraints.
Suppressing conflicting system records instead of preserving both states and escalating to the official owner.
Failing to preserve notice, hearing, appeal, objection, deadline, remedy, or adverse-action evidence where rights or obligations are affected.
Issuing procurement certification, audit findings, financial approvals, enforcement decisions, or legal interpretations rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.
Using generic program-management language that ignores public-service equity, statutory authority, official records, public transparency, or auditability constraints.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

required_workflow_questions.question: What statutory, eligibility, or due-process rules govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane is governed by tenant program statutes and agency policy, with public baseline guardrails from the APA for agency action, OMB A-11 Section 280 for public-service workflow expectations, 45 CFR 205.10 and 2 CFR Part 200 for hearing, appeal, remedy, and assistance-program examples. Eligibility and rights decisions are never inferred by the lane.

required_workflow_questions.question: What records must exist for the work to be auditable and fair?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane requires program authority, roster or application records, eligibility or participation file references, notices, correspondence, submission receipts, procurement-file references where applicable, records schedules, system timestamps, owner notes, access logs, and audit logs sufficient to reproduce status and owner decisions.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and adjudication, enforcement, or legal authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops when the action would decide eligibility, benefits, permits, sanctions, enforcement, hearing or appeal outcomes, legal meaning, procurement approval, public-records disclosure, record disposition, audit findings, financial approval, or policy override.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific program statutes and agency manuals vary widely
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant jurisdiction, program statute, agency manual, source hierarchy, authority map, and systems-of-record retrieval before final administrative use.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local public-records, privacy, retention, and appeal rules may be stricter than federal baselines
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Apply stricter tenant rules when supplied or retrieved; escalate conflicts instead of generalizing from the federal baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-specific procurement and financial-approval authority may be split across multiple offices
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant owner matrix and procurement or financial authority map before routing or evidence-package closure.

notes: The pack uses public federal baselines to constrain a generic government program administration lane.
Tenant-specific laws, policies, systems, records schedules, and authority boundaries remain mandatory runtime prerequisites rather than guessed pack content.
